title: heterotoma lobelioides
intro: Heterotoma lobelioides, also known as Lobelia or Pukatea, is a plant species native to New Zealand, belonging to the family Campanulaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Heterotoma lobelioides include relief from respiratory issues such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as its use in treating skin conditions and wounds. The plant can be prepared into various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
